title: Volunteer Tourism
description: For questions 9 – 16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Volunteer tourism, often called 'voluntourism', has (0) become increasingly popular in recent years. This form of travel combines vacation with volunteer work, allowing tourists to contribute to communities or environmental projects while experiencing new cultures.||Participants might work (9) on conservation projects, teach English in local schools, or help build infrastructure in developing communities. Advocates argue that volunteer tourism creates (10) a meaningful connection between visitors and local communities, providing benefits to both parties. Volunteers gain valuable life experience and cultural understanding, (11) while host communities receive much-needed support and resources.||However, critics raise concerns (12) about the effectiveness and ethics of short-term volunteer projects. They argue that volunteers often find (13) themselves unable to make a real difference due to insufficient training. In some cases, projects may be designed more to attract paying volunteers (14) than to address genuine local needs. There have been instances where poorly planned voluntourism has actually caused harm to communities or wildlife.||Experts recommend (15) that anyone considering volunteer tourism should research organisations carefully. Look for programmes that work (16) in close partnership with local communities and maintain a long-term commitment to sustainable development. The goal should be to create genuine positive impact rather than simply feeling good about helping.

example.text: Volunteer tourism, often called 'voluntourism', has (0) increasingly popular in recent years.

questions.explanation: Expresión 'work on projects' indica trabajar en proyectos específicos.
Conjunción 'while' contrasta los beneficios para diferentes grupos.
Expresión 'raise concerns about' significa expresar preocupaciones sobre algo.
Reflexive pronoun used in the expression 'find oneself', describing an unexpected or unintended situation.
Conjunción 'that' introduce una cláusula nominal después de 'recommend'.
Preposition used in the fixed expression 'in partnership with', indicating a collaborative relationship.
